role: Crown Prince of Iran (in exile); opposition leader advocating secular democratic transition
bio:
Reza Pahlavi is the eldest son of the late Mohammad Reza Shah Pahlavi and [Farah Pahlavi](/people/farah-pahlavi). He left Iran in 1978 to train as a fighter pilot in the United States and never returned. Following his father's death in Cairo in 1980, he declared himself Shah of Iran, though he later shifted his public stance toward advocacy for a secular democratic republic, positioning himself as a unifying figure for the Iranian opposition rather than claiming the throne.

He co-founded the Iran National Council (INC) in 2013 as a platform for coordinating opposition activities. During the 2022–2023 Woman, Life, Freedom protests, he emerged as one of the most visible exile voices, calling for civil disobedience and international support for the protest movement.

His father's former confidant, [Ahmad Ali Masoud Ansari](/people/ahmad-ali-ansari) (brother of historian [Ali M. Ansari](/people/ali-m-ansari)), has publicly alleged that Reza Pahlavi and his aides were funded by Saudi Arabia and the CIA — claims that Reza Pahlavi has not publicly addressed. He resides in Potomac, Maryland.
editorial_notes: Born 31 October 1960 in Tehran. Left Iran 1978. Declared himself Shah after father's death 1980. Co-founded Iran National Council 2013. Wife: Yasmine Etemad-Amini (married 1986). Three daughters. Lives in Potomac, Maryland. Ahmad Ali Masoud Ansari alleged Saudi/CIA funding — unverified.
